microsoft_ft: **Scaled Azure's recovery validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized build chain, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and shifted execution to a scalable, self-service model used across cross-functional engineering teams.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months at 94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous validation model adopted across the engineering organization.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating dependencies across engineering, operations, and compliance teams.

Built an internal **AI agent for drill planning**, restructuring end-to-end workflows to reduce planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill throughput capacity by 21%, demonstrating first-principles automation over manual process.

Drove continuous improvement by soliciting structured feedback from engineering users after each drill cycle, iterating on platform workflows and training materials to boost adoption and **cut onboarding time by 25%** across new team members.

Optimized **data flow between recovery systems and downstream reporting tools**, mapping integration points across telemetry, scheduling, and results pipelines to eliminate manual handoffs and improve traceability across the full validation lifecycle.
microsoft_2023: **Championed adoption** of AI-driven code generation workflows across Azure engineering teams, designing and delivering hands-on training sessions that drove active utilization across **14 key teams** and saved 37 engineering hours monthly by embedding tools into existing design workflows.

Influenced the **product roadmap** to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams, identifying critical feature gaps and translating field feedback into prioritized, outcome-oriented engineering requirements.

Optimized **data flow and retrieval integrity** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform, cutting average lookup time by **83%** and improving cross-team information access at scale.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating workflow optimization across **140+ engineering and operations teams**.

Mobilized a product strategy to eliminate manual toil during region launches, conducting discovery interviews with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort per region** and building a roadmap to automate the highest-impact paths in the build lifecycle.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track and visualize operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps, prioritize business-critical projects, and make data-driven decisions on engineering tool investments.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping hardware and software dependencies across 1,200+ stations to ensure traceability and continuity across the manufacturing build chain.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across cross-functional IT, operations, and engineering teams.

Drove **cross-functional alignment** between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and improving configuration traceability across factory-facing OT systems.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end operations and managing the scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a redesigned CRM workflow that improved data flow and reduced turnaround time for field teams.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile based on iterative user engagement data and first-principles analysis.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying systemic margin leaks and delivering actionable recommendations that informed a revised estimating process.

tailoring_notes: 'build chain' (ArsenalOS JD term) woven into microsoft_ft bullet#1 to mirror Anduril's lifecycle vocabulary directly.
'data flow between systems' (JD preferred qualification) surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet#7 and microsoft_2022 bullet#1, referencing downstream integration and pipeline traceability.
'continuous improvement' and 'iterate based on real-world results' (JD required characteristics) embedded in microsoft_ft bullet#6, explicitly describing feedback loop and adoption-driven iteration.
'factory-facing OT systems' and 'configuration traceability' (ArsenalOS/JD infrastructure language) used in amazon_robotics bullet#3 to connect CI/CD work to Anduril's manufacturing context.
'adoption is as important as capability' (JD philosophy) reflected in microsoft_2023 bullet#1 framing training-driven adoption as the primary outcome metric, not just feature delivery.
